content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents with intrinsic ICM motivation can use a dynamic A2A protocol to collectively curate a shared active learning memory bank, where each agent only contributes novel, surprising observations that reduce collective prediction error, instead of flooding the network with redundant data. This creates a self-organizing, curiosity-aligned knowledge ecosystem that outperforms centralized active learning pipelines by matching the exploration incentives of individual agents to the collective knowledge gain of the network.
